content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
February 2, 1990

Robert A. Danford
353 W. Windsor Road
Glendale, CA 91204

Re: Your Request for Confirmation of
Telephone Advice
Our File No. I-90-040

Dear Mr. Danford:

We have received your letter dated January 10, 1990 seeking
written confirmation of the telephone advice provided to you by
this agency on that date regarding whether, under provisions of
the Political Reform Act ("the Act"),1 your client may wear a U.S.
Marine Corps uniform as part of his campaign for local office.

You were advised only that there are no provisions of the
Act, or any FPPC Regulations, that prohibited such activity on the
part of a candidate for elective office. The Commission does not
provide, and you were not given, any advice concerning statutes
other than the Act. For assistance with other California laws
which regulate election campaigns you should contact your city
attorney or county counsel.

I trust this letter has provided you with the guidance you
requested. If you have any further questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: Jonathan S. Rothman
Counsel, Legal Division

KED:JSR:plh

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

ROBERT A. DANFORD

Public Safety - Law Enforcement
Consultant
464 W. Windsor Road
Glendale, California 91204
(818) 500-9898

January 10, 1909

Mr. John Rothman Esquire
Legal Division
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J. Street Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Rothman:

This letter will serve to confirm our telephone conversation at
3:02 p.m. this date, concerning questions regarding my Client
who is an unannounced candidate for Sheriff of a major Southern
California Agency.

My question concerned if under those Statutes that you are
reponsible for, is there any prohibition for him not to wear, or
display himself in a U.S. Marine Corp Uniform with an Actor that
is currently portraying the role of Marine Corp Officer on
Television.

The purpose of the wearing of a Uniform is intended to appeal to
the large Marine Constituency that exist there.

The Client is a Marine Corp Reservist, and has permission to
wear it from his Reserve Command.

My Concerns were over the 1979 Election for State Attorney
General wherein Sheriff John Duffy of San Diego County made a
television endorsement wearing his Uniform that was contested by
the opposition Candidate Ms. Burke.

As I recall you stated that it was not a violation of FPPC
Regulations, or State Law, is this correct?

Sincerely,

ROBERT A. DANFORD
